title: GETDOCUMENT
text: GETDOCUMENT Retrieves a maximum of 10240000 characters of the XML stream generated thus far. This call can be repeated to obtain larger documents. Explain this code GETDOCUMENT(XmlDocument, Keep) Copy Parameters Table 1. GETDOCUMENT parameters Parameter Data Type Direction Description XmlDocument String OUT Named location of the retrieved XML string. The maximum number of characters that can be returned is 10240000. However, since half UTF8 characters are not returned, the actual length may be a few bytes less than 10240000, even though more data is available. A character that does not entirely fit at the end will be returned as the first character the next time GETDOCUMENT is called. Keep Boolean IN Indicates whether the XML document returned is kept in memory. If GETDOCUMENT is invoked again, it returns the document in memory, which may have been extended if other UXMLWRITER operations have been invoked. Return Values Values returned: >= 0 Number of bytes that remain to be fetched. This is the number of bytes that the next call to GETDOCUMENT will return. Description This operation can be invoked at any time to retrieve the current contents of the XML document, or it can be invoked at the end, after the ENDDOCUMENT operation, when the document is complete. The XML text constructed up to this point is copied to the XmlDocument parameter, which can then be stored in a database, dumped or appended to an XML file using filedump , or sent to a 3rd-party application. For XML documents smaller than 10240000 bytes, you can generate the entire document, and afterwards call GETDOCUMENT once to retrieve the entire document. In this case, the value of the Keep parameter makes no difference. For documents larger than 10240000 bytes, you need to call GETDOCUMENT several times. Using the value of the return value, you can construct a loop that calls GETDOCUMENT until all of the XML string has been retrieved. For example: Explain this code ; Document 3 is more than 10240 bytes: $1 = uxml3->getdocument(FIELD1, "true") filedump FIELD1, "file.xml" while ($1 > 0) $1 = uxml3->getdocument(FIELD1, "true") filedump/append FIELD1, "file.xml" endwhile Copy This loop retrieves the entire document, concatenating all the pieces into file file.xml . Since Keep is true , you can redo this loop and retrieve the same document, for example, into a second XML file. For large XML documents, this approach requires a large amount of memory, because the entire XML document has to be kept in memory so that it can be retrieved in its entirety when the document is finished. To use memory more economically, set Keep to false . You can then retrieve a smaller intermediate piece of the document with GETDOCUMENT , discard that piece of XML, and then continue to generate more XML, reusing the memory that was occupied by the previous piece. The total memory footprint is determined by the largest piece between two consecutive calls of GETDOCUMENT . If an intermediate piece of XML can still be larger than 10240000 bytes, then you must still retrieve that piece using a loop, and only the last call to GETDOCUMENT determines whether the document piece is kept in memory for a second retrieval or not. The GETDOCUMENT operation does not free the memory occupied by the XML text. The Keep parameter only determines whether or not to reuse the memory held by the previous piece of XML, if that piece must be returned again. The memory is freed only when the UXMLWRITER instance is deleted, which is done using deleteinstance . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
